title: -Contract: InfoSend for Bill Printing and Mailing Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a piggyback contract and
any amendments with InfoSend in an amount not to exceed $450,000 for bill printing and
mailing services from July 30, 2025 through July 30, 2028.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The cost will be $150,000 per year over 3 years for a total contract amount NTE $450,000
and will be funded by the General Fund and the Zero Waste Fund. The year one funds
are available in the FY2026 budget.
sections.current_situation:
The Revenue Collection Division is responsible for maintaining customer accounts as well
as printing and mailing thousands of bills on a regular basis to a wide range of City
customers. Currently, many of these bills—including those for Zero Waste, Environmental
Health, the Marina, and the Rental Housing Safety Program—are processed by an
outside vendor. In addition, tens of thousands of business license tax forms are mailed to
customers annually.
The Finance Department researched vendors that could perform the scope after receiving
no adequate response from a formal solicitation. It was determined that InfoSend had
numerous contracts with many municipalities that was competitively bid with competitive
pricing.
sections.background:
The City of Brawley conducted a competitive Request for Proposal (RFP) process that
solicited bids for a vendor to provide “Utility Bill Printing & Mailing Services”, which opened
on November 1, 2022, and closed on December 2, 2022. InfoSend was selected as the
most responsive vendor and was awarded a base contract period that runs from February
7, 2023, through June 30, 2026 with automatic renewals for successive two (2) year

sections.rationale:
The Finance Department researched vendors that could perform the scope after receiving
no adequate response from a formal solicitation. It was determined that InfoSend had
numerous contracts with many municipalities that was competitively bid with competitive
pricing.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This program will provide flexibility to the Finance Department to have multiple vendors
to print and mail bills to customers. Unlike other print vendors, this one is located in
California and has the same operating hours and printing facilities within the Pacific
Service Time zone.
As this vendor will mail bills from within California, there will be an increase in
environmental sustainability and will help mitigate the effects of climate change.
CONTACT PERSON
Henry Oyekanmi, Director of Finance, Finance Department, 981-7301
sections.attachments:
1. Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: INFOSEND BILLING PRINTING AND MAILING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the Revenue Collection Division in the Finance Department is responsible
for maintaining customer accounts as well as printing and mailing thousands of bills on a
regular basis to a wide range of City customers; and
WHEREAS, the Finance Department researched vendors that could perform the scope
after receiving no adequate response from a formal solicitation and it was determined that
InfoSend had numerous contracts with many municipalities that was competitively bid
with competitive pricing; and
WHEREAS, unlike many printing companies, InfoSend is located in California. By
decreasing the distance the mail will have to travel, it will decrease its effect on climate
change; and
WHEREAS, the City of Brawley, in Southern California, conducted a competitive Request
for Proposal (RFP) process that solicited bids for a vendor to provide “Utility Billing and
Printing Services”, which opened on November 1, 2022 and closed on December 2, 2022.
InfoSend was selected as the most responsive vendor and was awarded a base contract
period that runs from February 7, 2023, through June 30, 2026 with automatic renewals
for successive two (2) year periods unless either Client or InfoSend provides the other
party with at least sixty (60) days’ written notice prior to the end of the current term; and
WHEREAS, the Finance Department is requesting to piggyback off the City of Brawley
Contract for the same services using funds from the General Fund and the Zero Waste
Fund.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is authorized to execute a piggyback contract and any
amendments with InfoSend for $150,000 per year, in an amount not to exceed $450,000
for bill printing and mailing services from July 30, 2025 through July 30, 2028
